entry.title: SAP implementation: what should we get right before we start?
entry.content:
I wanna say that ur business has been growing at a pretty fast pace, and that’s always good news. However, our infrastructure hasn’t exactly been growing at that same pace. Currently, we have our finance, our orders, our client information, and our logistics information. But, it’s starting to look like we’re one bad sync away from total chaos.

So, we’re actually considering implementing SAP and bringing everything under one roof and making it scalable. While I think it’s a great idea in theory, I also understand that it’s something that can go wrong if it’s not set up properly from the get-go.

So, for those who have gone through this in the past, what are the non-negotiables that need to be in place before you ever start? What did you find had the biggest impact in terms of actually getting it up and running without it turning into a never-ending project?

entry.title: Re: NO Foreign trade reporting in the public cloud (like...
entry.content:
Good day @Angela2 

Thank you for your question.

You are probably not missing a hidden classic foreign trade reporting feature. The Public Cloud approaches this differently: It provides country or region-specific Balance of Payments/Central Bank Payment statutory reports in Run Statutory Reports, and you should, where possible, automatically derive reporting attributes such as LZB or SCB indicator, not enforce only through manual entry plus validation.

So, what I recommend is:

  1. Verify whether your requirement report is already available in Run Statutory Report
  2. Implement derivation logic for the LZB or SCB indicator and supplying country based on the guidance mentioned in KBA 2845258
  3. Keep the validation rule only as your fallback control option
  4. Avoid spending time looking for an SCB indicator maintenance in SSCUI in Public Cloud, because this maintenance option is not available.

Here are references for more guidance

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/6833660c7722498fb41584b098420f89/556604558d6447779076737a0de21764.html?locale=en-US

3486912 - Unable to create/maintain SCB Indicator - SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition - SAP for Me

Let me know if you have further questions.

Best regards

Chris

entry.title: SuccessFactors Compensation - Move employee's compensation data from old manager to current manager
entry.content:
Hello everyone,

Customer has the following issue: Due to organizational reshuffling, employees have been assigned to new managers. These new employees are not shown in the completed compensation forms (Tab: Salary Planning) of the previous compensation cycle (completed at the end of 2025). Strangely, some employees that have been newly assigned show on the worksheet, others don't. Is this expected behaviour? (FYI: Managers cannot use Executive Review in this instance.)

I have tried the following things in a test environment and rebuilt the scenario there (unfortunately, test and prod differ very much) - meaning having two completed worksheets there (not published) and then reassigning an employee to a new manager.

  • Update all worksheets: Move employee's compensation data from old manager to current manager.
    -> this does nothing
  • Update a specific worksheet: Changes in eligibility and add newly eligible employees to compensation worksheet.
    -> also nothing happens
  • Moving employees can only be done for open worksheets.

In this test scenario, the manager had access to Executive Review and here the newly assigned employee was visible, however not in the tab Salary Planning.

When I relaunched the two worksheets and reapplied the eligibility rules first, it worked.

I hope I could clearly state my problem, essentially the question is how to move employee's compensation data from old manager to current manager when the compensation cycle is already done.

Thanks in advance for your help!

Best regards

entry.title: 1H 2026 Product Release - EC - Latest People Profile - New Edit & History UI
entry.content:
We have observed that the latest People Profile introduces stricter rules and validation compared to the previous version. Since adopting the new Edit & History UI, our company is encountering a critical issue that directly impacts daily operations.

Issue description

  • When editing an employee record in the new Edit & History UI (for example, updating the Cost Center),
  • Other mandatory fields unexpectedly become empty.
  • When those empty fields are reselected or corrected, additional fields become empty again.
  • This cycle makes it extremely difficult and error-prone to save valid changes.

Impact

  • The issue significantly blocks administrative maintenance of Employee Central data.
  • We cannot simplify or relax our business rules or object definitions, as they are essential for system integrity and downstream processes.

Question

  • Is SAP aware of this behavior in the new Edit & History UI?
  • Will SAP investigate this issue and provide a fix or guidance on how this should be handled?

SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central - Employee Profile

entry.title: File Not Received in SAP PO After Server Change – Sender Channel Conflict Issue
entry.content:
Scenario Overview:

In our integration landscape, the data flow is:

AccordFT (MFT) → FTP → SAP PO → File (Archival) → SAP PO → SAP CAR/POSDTA

We are using a single standard interface and mapping for all countries. File processing is dynamic, and files are handled based on the country code.The MFT team confirmed that SAF files were successfully processed and sent from their end. However, SAP PO did not receive the files for one specific country, and the data was not delivered to the SAP CAR system.

Issue:

  • Files were successfully sent from MFT
  • No messages were visible in SAP PO for one country
  • Other country files were processed successfully using the same interface
  • Data was not reaching SAP CAR

Additionally, the issue was difficult to trace because:

  • No explicit errors were observed
  • Configuration appeared correct and consistent
    Cause
  • The issue occurred after replacing the old PO server with a new server in the ICO configuration.

The scenario was successfully tested in QA without any issues, as the system was still pointing to the old server. This created a false positive validation, and the issue only surfaced in Production after switching to the new server.
QA:

In Production:

  • The configuration was updated to use the new server
  • The issue appeared only after this change

Further analysis revealed that:

  • There were only two ICOs and two sender channels with similar configurations
    These existed because:
  • One set was originally used for the old server
  • Another set was created for the new server configuration
  • After replacing the old server with the new one, both configurations became effectively identical, leading to overlap

Additionally, the interface design included connected flows:

  • One flow handles file pickup and archival in SAP PO
  • Another flow processes and delivers data to SAP CAR

Due to similar sender channel configurations (same server and path, different users), one channel interfered with file pickup. Because of the connected interface design:

  • The file was not picked correctly in the inbound flow
  • As a result, downstream processing to SAP CAR did not occur
  • Since the configurations were nearly identical and no errors were thrown, the issue was not immediately visible.

Solution:

To resolve the issue:

  • Reviewed all sender channels and ICOs for overlapping configurations
  • Identified the duplicate/conflicting sender channel
  • Stopped the unnecessary sender channel
  • Requested the MFT team to resend the SAF file

After implementing the fix:

  • SAP PO successfully picked up the file
  • The archival flow executed correctly
  • The file was processed and delivered to SAP CAR

    Key Takeaways:
  • Avoid maintaining duplicate ICOs and sender channels with identical configurations
  • Ensure clear separation when transitioning from old to new servers
  • Validate end-to-end flows, especially when interfaces are interconnected (archival + processing flows)
  • Always test using the actual target server (Production-like setup)
  • Similar configurations without errors can still cause silent processing conflicts

entry.title: Re: AI driven Composite Creation in SAP Analytics Cloud using SAP AI Core
entry.content:
Hi @zee_siddique15,

Once you add the custom widget to the story, make sure to use the same dataset that was originally uploaded for the widget.

Also, ensure that the measures and dimensions are added in the exact order specified in the JSON file (under the MyDataBinding section). This ordering is important for the widget to interpret the data correctly.

The widget should automatically infer the timeline (including where the forecast begins) based on the data itself, so you typically won’t need to manually provide a “Forecast Start Date” within SAC.

entry.title: Auto-Populating the External Actual Transfer of Control Date on POD Confirmation
entry.content:
Introduction

In the 5MQ (Intercompany Sales – Delivery-Based) scenario in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, the External Transfer of Control Date on the outbound delivery plays a critical role in intercompany billing accuracy. This date signals the moment when legal ownership of goods transfers to the external party — a key input for revenue recognition and intercompany invoice generation.

By default, the Actual Date under Header Details → Shipment → External Transfer of Control Dates must be entered manually by the warehouse or logistics team. This introduces the risk of delays, inconsistencies, or simply being forgotten.

This blog post describes a clean BAdI-based enhancement that automatically sets the Actual Date to the current system date the moment a warehouse operator confirms the Proof of Delivery (POD) in the Outbound Delivery change transaction.

Business Requirement

Scenario: 5MQ – Intercompany Sales with Outbound Delivery

Trigger: The user opens an outbound delivery, navigates to the Overview POD tab, checks "Confirm Proof of Delivery", and clicks Save.

Desired behavior: At the moment of Save, the field:

Header Details → Shipment tab → External Transfer of Control Dates → Actual Date

…should be automatically populated with today's system date.

This eliminates manual entry, ensures audit-trail consistency, and aligns the transfer of control date with the POD confirmation event.

Technical Approach: BAdI LE_SHP_MODIFY_HEAD

SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition provides the extensibility BAdI LE_SHP_MODIFY_HEAD (Modify Outbound Delivery Header) through the Custom Logic app (transaction: Manage Your Solution → Adapt Logic). This BAdI fires during delivery processing whenever the delivery header data is modified — including during POD confirmation.

Interface parameters of interest:

Parameter Direction Description

delivery_document_inImportCurrent state of the delivery header
delivery_document_outExportModified delivery header to be applied
messagesExportMessages to display to the user

Key field:

 
INTCOEXTACTLTRANSFOFCTRLDTETME
Description : External Actual Transfer of Control Date
Type        : Packed number, Length 8 (TIMESTAMP format: YYYYMMDDHHMMSS)


Implementation

Step 1 – Navigate to Custom Logic

Go to: Manage Your Solution → Adapt Logic → search for BAdI LE_SHP_MODIFY_HEAD → Create Implementation.

Step 2 – Write the Enhancement Code

Paste the following code into the Custom Logic editor and click Save and Publish:

 
" Always copy input to output first (mandatory pattern)
delivery_document_out = delivery_document_in.

" Only trigger when POD is confirmed (status = 'C')
IF delivery_document_in-overallproofofdeliverystatus = 'C'.

  " Build YYYYMMDDHHMMSS timestamp from current system date
  " Note: cl_abap_context_info=>get_system_date() returns an ordinal
  " in ABAP Cloud – we must use ISO string formatting to get YYYYMMDD
  DATA(lv_date_str) = |{ cl_abap_context_info=>get_system_date( ) DATE = ISO }|.
  REPLACE ALL OCCURRENCES OF '-' IN lv_date_str WITH ''.
  " Result: '20260331'

  DATA lv_ts TYPE timestamp.
  lv_ts = |{ lv_date_str }000000|.
  " Result: 20260331000000 (midnight UTC of the current date)

  " Write the timestamp to the External Actual Transfer of Control Date
  delivery_document_out-intcoextactltransfofctrldtetme = lv_ts.

ENDIF.

Step 3 – Test

  1. Open an outbound delivery in the POD change transaction.
  2. Check "Confirm Proof of Delivery" → POD Status changes to C (Confirmed).
  3. Click Save.
  4. Navigate to Header Details → Shipment tab → External Transfer of Control Dates.
  5. Verify that Actual Date is now populated with today's date.

 

Key Technical Learnings

This implementation required resolving several non-obvious challenges:

1. Always assign delivery_document_out = delivery_document_in first

The BAdI output parameter starts empty. Failing to copy the input first will wipe all delivery header data. This is the mandatory first line of any LE_SHP_MODIFY_HEAD implementation.

2. Check overallproofofdeliverystatus, not mode

The BAdI does not have a dedicated "POD confirmation" mode flag. The correct way to detect the confirmation event is to check:

 
delivery_document_in-overallproofofdeliverystatus = 'C'

The BAdI fires on every user interaction (including simply opening the delivery in change mode), so this condition scopes the logic to the correct moment.

3. cl_abap_context_info=>get_system_date() returns an ordinal number

In ABAP Cloud, TYPE d stores dates as ordinal day counters (e.g., 739707 for March 31, 2026), not as the classic YYYYMMDD character string. Directly assigning this to a packed-number timestamp field stores an incorrect value that the UI cannot interpret — resulting in a blank display.

The correct pattern is to use the ISO date template literal:

 
DATA(lv_date_str) = |{ cl_abap_context_info=>get_system_date( ) DATE = ISO }|.
" Produces: '2026-03-31' (semantic string, not ordinal)

This leverages ABAP's string formatting to produce the human-readable ISO date regardless of internal representation.

4. The field INTCOEXTACTLTRANSFOFCTRLDTETME is a TIMESTAMP (P LENGTH 8), not a DATE

Despite appearing as a "date" field in the UI, the underlying type is a 14-digit packed timestamp (YYYYMMDDHHMMSS). Writing only the 8-digit date portion (20260331) results in an invalid date — the system interprets it as year=0002, month=02, day=60, which renders as blank.

The correct value must include a time component:

 
lv_ts = |{ lv_date_str }000000|.  " → 20260331000000

Note on timezone: Writing 000000 (midnight UTC) will display as 08:00 in a UTC+8 system — this is expected timezone conversion behavior and is perfectly acceptable for date-stamping purposes.

Summary

Aspect Detail
BAdILE_SHP_MODIFY_HEAD
Trigger conditionoverallproofofdeliverystatus = 'C'
Target fieldINTCOEXTACTLTRANSFOFCTRLDTETME
Field typeTYPE timestamp (P LENGTH 8, YYYYMMDDHHMMSS format)
Date retrievalcl_abap_context_info=>get_system_date( ) with DATE = ISO formatting
Value written20260331000000 (date + 000000 for time)

This implementation is fully cloud-compliant (no restricted APIs, no direct DB access), requires no transport, and is immediately active after publishing through the Custom Logic app.

Tested on: SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, 2602 release

entry.title: SAC Modeler – Split alphanumeric IDs into “letters” and “digits” when the prefix length varies
entry.content:
Context

  • SAP Analytics Cloud, New Model (data imported from Excel), working in Modeler > Transform Data / calculated columns.

Data/Goal

  • One column contains IDs like: ABC12345, WXYZ1234, etc.
  • I need two new columns:
    • Letters: the leading alphabetic characters
    • Numbers: the trailing digits
  • Expected examples: ABC12345 → ABC | 12345; WXYZ1234 → WXYZ | 1234.
  • The count of letters varies (3–4 or more), and there is no delimiter.

What I tried

  • Split transform: can’t find a delimiter that separates letters from digits.
  • SUBSTR/LENGTH: works only when the length is constant; with variable lengths it produces wrong results (e.g., returns 'Z1234' for WXYZ1234). 

 Questions

  1. What is the recommended way in SAC Modeler to split these IDs into “letters” and “digits” when the prefix length varies?

entry.title: Re: Receipt Management - Irritants to follow the receipts...
entry.content:
Good day @Romane_QUAGLIARINI 

Thank you for your question.

The best answer to your situation is usually not a single app, but you will have to combine:

  • A monitoring app for your open or overdue PO items
  • A simple app for posting the goods receipt, and
  • If you need, a notification mechanism so users are alerted before the delay becomes critical.

On the question if there is an app to follow receipts easily, the answer is Yes. The most useful standard apps for that are:

  • Monitor Purchase Order Items, which helps you to monitor PO items by supplier, purchaser, plant, material, and status. The app helps you manage your operational needs for purchase order item statuses.
  • Overdue Purchase Order Items, which you can use to analyse your PO items whose delivery date is passed. Provides details on monthly or urgent backlog view.
  • My Purchasing Document Items-Professional gives your users an overview of purchasing document items by supplier and helps them keep track of purchasing documents.
  • Post Goods Receipt for Purchasing Document app helps you execute your actual receipt posting against the PO.

So yes, there are standard apps to monitor and process receipts. The gap is usually not missing functionality but that users are not yet working with the right operational worklist.

 

The likely reason why your invoice posting is blocked is that the PO item is using a GR Based Invoice Verification. When that indicator is active, the system expects you to post goods receipt before you can post invoice for the received quantity. See KBA 3265568 for more details.

So, this is not usually a bug. The system sets that control logic. So, what you should improve in your process is to make missing GR visible earlier and easier to act on.

My recommendation is to use a receipt monitoring worklist as the daily operational cockpit. The best option here is to use the Monitor Purchase Order Items app. It enables displaying and monitoring purchase order items with relevant filters and status information.

  • In the Monitor Purchase Order Items app, you can filter Supplier, Purchasing Group, Plant, PO, Material and save it as a variant for each team or user group. For example, due this month, Receipts overdue, My Purchasing group receipts, etc.

  • You can review the item status list (or PO items) and identify items that are still pending receipt

  • You can navigate from the selected item to the PO and then to the posting app if you need to enter receipts.

This helps you by creating a clear operational queue instead of asking users to search PO by PO.

Consult this reference for more details.

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/0e602d466b99490187fcbb30d1dc897c/82f1e2574096f432e10000000a441470.html?locale=en-US

 

There is also the option to proactively activate notifications instead of waiting for month end. SAP offers the option of to manage Situation Handling for overdue purchase order items. That means you can notify your relevant users when PO items are overdue instead of relying only on manual reporting.

Consult these references for more details.

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/0e602d466b99490187fcbb30d1dc897c/c290ae0c48c644f78039dfd53fd4b6be.html?locale=en-US

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/0e602d466b99490187fcbb30d1dc897c/b50656c4de48492e8ea1a528a7492d3b.html?locale=en-US

 

You mentioned that “if no receipt is put on the purchase order, it is impossible to put an invoice on the purchase order”. That is often correct when GR Based IV is active. So, I would also recommend checking whether all your PO categories really need GR Base IV.

 

Let me know if you have further questions.

Best regards

Chris

entry.title: Issue with EC New UI – MDF Object Composite Level Limitation (Error 400)
entry.content:
Hello Everyone,

I am currently facing an issue after enabling the new Employee Central (EC) UI in our test environment. Our SuccessFactors module/portlet is not functioning as expected.

The UI displays only the portlet name and throws the following error:

Error Message:

"The object cust_EmployeesAttachment is inaccessible. Ensure that the object has a maximum of 2 composite levels and try again."

Error Code: 400

At present, our MDF object is configured with 4 composite levels (Parent, Child, Grandchild, Grand grandchild object) However, reducing it to 2 levels is not a straightforward option, as it introduces inconsistencies and impacts the UI behavior. Additionally, this change would involve a complex reconfiguration effort.

The issue remains unresolved, and we are unable to proceed further.

Has anyone encountered a similar limitation with the new EC UI? Any suggestions, workarounds, or best practices would be greatly appreciated.

Thank you in advance for your support!

entry.title: Research Survey: Building an AI Agent for Intelligent Order Combination - Your SAP Expertise Needed!
entry.content:
Order management is one of the most critical yet complex processes in any SAP environment. Whether you're dealing with purchase orders, sales orders or production orders, the challenge of efficiently combining and optimizing these orders while maintaining accuracy and compliance is something every SAP professional faces daily. We're in the early stages of exploring how AI could transform this process and we need your expertise to guide us in the right direction.

What We're Building

The Challenge

Currently, order combination processes often involve:

  • Manual review and decision-making that's time-consuming and error-prone
  • Complex business rules that vary significantly between organizations
  • Integration challenges across different SAP modules and systems
  • Difficulty balancing efficiency gains with accuracy requirements
  • Limited visibility into optimization opportunities
Our Vision

We're exploring the development of an AI agent that could assist with intelligent order combination by:

  • Understanding your specific business rules and constraints
  • Learning from historical order patterns and outcomes
  • Suggesting optimal combinations based on multiple criteria
  • Integrating seamlessly with your existing SAP processes
  • Providing transparent reasoning for its recommendations

The goal isn't to replace human judgment, but to augment it with intelligent insights that help you make better, faster decisions.

Why We Need YOU

Your Experience Matters

We're seeking input from SAP professionals who work with order processes daily:

  • Procurement specialists and buyers
  • Sales order management teams
  • Production planners and schedulers
  • SAP functional consultants
  • Business analysts working with order-to-cash or procure-to-pay processes
  • Anyone involved in order optimization and combination decisions
What We Want to Learn

Your real-world experience is invaluable for understanding:

  • Current pain points and manual workarounds in order combination
  • Business rules and criteria that drive combination decisions
  • Integration requirements with existing SAP systems
  • User interface preferences and workflow needs
  • Success metrics and quality measures that matter most
  • Potential concerns or barriers to AI adoption in this area

About This Survey

Participation Details
  • Time Required: 8-12 minutes
  • Confidentiality: All responses are completely anonymous
  • Who Should Participate: SAP professionals involved in any aspect of order management, combination or optimization
What We're Asking About
  • Your current order combination processes and challenges
  • Decision criteria and business rules you use today
  • Desired features and capabilities for an AI assistant
  • Implementation preferences and success factors

Take the Survey

Survey Link - Share Your Experience Here

Your insights will directly shape how we approach this requirements engineering effort and ensure any future solution addresses real business needs rather than theoretical problems.

Join the Conversation

Have additional thoughts about AI in order management? Share them in the comments below! We'd love to hear about specific use cases, success stories or concerns you might have. Please also share this survey with colleagues who work with order processes - the more diverse perspectives we gather, the better we can understand the full scope of requirements.

Thank You

Thank you for taking the time to share your expertise with us. Requirements engineering is most successful when it's grounded in real user needs and experiences and your participation makes that possible.

For questions about this research or to discuss order management challenges in your environment, feel free to reach out through the comments or SAP Community messaging.

 

 

 

Tags

#artificial-intelligence #order-management #requirements-engineering #business-process-optimization #user-research #sap-integration

entry.title: Re: questions about x-csrf-token for SAP OData APIs
entry.content:
Hi,

1. Under normal circumstances, in SAP S/4HANA Cloud, the validity period of a x-csrf-token is half an hour. However, this is also related to the session validity period.

2. This can be viewed through the APP Message Dashboard in Public Cloud system. Note that inbound communication logs are only visible when errors occur. Also, you need to assign user as recipient to receive message in the APP Assign Recipients to Users.

3. A x-csrf-token is essentially a protection mechanism against cross-site request forgery, so if requests are sent through platforms like Feishu, the x-csrf-token cannot be bypassed.  In Public Cloud system's Test Automation tool, sending OData POST requests does not require configuring a x-csrf-token, but this is because Test Automation is a cloud-native application. For errors on the Feishu platform, it’s recommended to try troubleshooting methods such as clearing cookies, checking sessions. Under normal circumstances, the x-csrf-token logic should be supported.

Best Regards,

Zhehui

entry.title: Migration Notice - Moving Forward Together: LAC, Spain & Portugal join Global SAP Community soon!
entry.content:
Dear community members from our regional group LAC, Spain and Portugal,

To strengthen collaboration and reduce duplicated efforts, we will be merging our regional community "Latin America & Caribbean, Spain and Portugal" into the recently created Global - SAP Community. Going forward, regional posts, discussions and events will take place in the global group directly so we can improve visibility, share knowledge more broadly, and create better synergies.

What will happen

Existing discussions, blogposts, and events from the regional group will be moved to the global group of SAP University Alliances & SAP Young Thinkers

Timing
We plan to start the migration in the coming weeks and expect the move to be completed asap.

How you can help or what to expect

Please follow or join the global group now so you don’t miss updates: Global - SAP Community

Questions or concerns

If you have any comments or if you need assistance, leave your message in the comments feature below or write to us via universityalliances@sap.com

Thank you for your cooperation as we make this transition to better serve the community.

Best regards,

SAP Academic Partnerships – LAC, Portugal and Spain

entry.title: SAP Fiori Tools 2603 Update: RAP Project Accelerator, Walkthrough, Adaptation Improvements, and More
entry.content:
With SAP Fiori tools 2603, we deliver a set of focused improvements across development transparency, onboarding, AI-supported development, and extensibility. Highlights include Project Accelerator support for RAP, a change in navigation from properties to its source, a new walkthrough in Visual Studio Code, and the ability to import existing key user changes into an SAPUI5 Adaptation Project.

Project Accelerator for RAP generates full-stack SAP Fiori elements applications from text or images

One of the most important additions in this release is the extension of the Project Accelerator to the ABAP RESTful Application Programming Model (RAP). The Project Accelerator has been available for SAP Cloud Application Programming Model (CAP) scenarios since 2024 and supports rapid prototyping with AI, based on images and/or a requirements description. With release 2603, it can now also create ABAP artifacts and services with AI assistance, addressing a long-standing request.

This significantly broadens the reach of the Project Accelerator for enterprise SAP development. The feature is available for cloud-based RAP back ends with SAP BTP or SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2602 or higher. For more information please refer to the SAP Help Portal page Project Accelerator Using SAP Fiori Tools AI. A separate blog post dedicated to this addition is currently in preparation and will be linked here once published.

The Project Accelerator lets you generate applications directly from business requirements documents, either text or images.

 

 

 

 

The Project Accelerator for RAP builds on the capabilities already available for CAP.

 

Direct navigation to source in the manifest gives developers a more transparent editing experience

Since the beginning, SAP Fiori tools has followed a clear principle: support development tasks with convenience, but do not hide the underlying technical details from experienced developers. This is why properties and settings in the tooling generally allow navigation to the corresponding source. For annotations and XML-based settings, this already opens the relevant annotation or fragment file directly. For manifest-related changes, however, SAP Fiori tools previously used a simplified virtual representation of the manifest as an intermediate editing layer.

Over time, this abstraction became more of a liability than a benefit. It introduced additional complexity, made parts of the implementation more error-prone, and did not provide enough added value compared to working directly with the actual manifest.json. With release 2603, source links for manifest properties now open the corresponding location in the real manifest and highlight the relevant section directly. This gives developers a more transparent editing experience and simplifies the path from visual tooling to source code. It also creates room for future optimizations in areas such as undo/redo handling and patch logic, which are planned for a later release.

New walkthrough for getting started makes novice developers more productive more quickly

Users who already work with SAP Fiori tools often appreciate the convenience and breadth of what the tooling offers. At the same time, the number of available capabilities can make it harder for newcomers to find a good starting point. To make onboarding easier, we have added a new walkthrough in Visual Studio Code that helps users get started with SAP Fiori tools directly inside the IDE.

The walkthrough provides a practical entry point into the most relevant concepts and flows. It guides users through the initial setup and encourages hands-on exploration of the tooling. If you are new to SAP Fiori tools, or if you want a structured way to revisit the basics, try it out and complete the tasks.

The walkthrough can be started from the Help menu in VS Code, choose Open Walkthrough… and then SAP Fiori tools from the drop-down menu.

The SAP Fiori tools walkthrough explains all the capabilities and has hands-on opportunities to explore and learn.

Import key user changes into an Adaptation Project and keep your edits

With this release, when creating an SAPUI5 Adaptation Project, you can now import existing key user changes. This helps ensure that changes made through key user adaptation are not lost when moving to developer extensibility. The feature is available starting with SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2602.

For more background, read about key user adaptation and the transition to developer extensibility.

Additional new features in SAP Fiori tools 2603 and more planned

Release 2603 includes an update of the tools and generator to support ESLint v9. In parallel, we are internally testing a new validation feature that allows more flexible, domain-specific rules and visual integration of fix options into the Page Map and Page Editor. We plan to release this together with an initial ruleset in one of the upcoming SAP Fiori tools releases and cover it in a separate blog post.

There is also an improved support for value help services referenced by an app’s main OData V4 service. Their metadata can now be retrieved during app generation, which enables SAP Fiori tools to support scenarios such as property configuration for these value helps and mock data creation for preview. This work is part of a broader rework of test generation support that we plan to cover in a future release blog.

The MCP server for SAP Fiori can now generate an SAP Fiori elements application for an existing OData service, including services exposed from a RAP back end. It uses saved system connections from the Connection Manager extension together with a system-relative service URI. This means that the authentication flow can remain outside the AI client and the LLM, following the established SAP system sign-in flow. This is one step toward end-to-end, AI-driven, RAP-based application development in Visual Studio Code, and we will continue to expand this path in upcoming releases.

If you want to stay up to date with changes in SAP Fiori tools between release blog posts, the What’s New section in the SAP Help Portal remains the best place to track newly delivered capabilities and fixes.

The SAP Fiori development portal includes explanations and sample code for all the building blocks available for creating SAP Fiori elements applications.

entry.title: Is it possible to combine a filter with the exportPDF scripts in SAC?
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

I’m using exportPDF scripting behind a button to automatically export story PDFs in SAC. Since I have around 100 PDFs to export, and each one takes about 2–3 minutes, the entire process takes roughly four hours to complete.

One limitation is that if the export process gets interrupted, restarting it will always begin from the very first file again, rather than resuming from where it stopped.

Given this constraint, I’m wondering if it is possible to add a “filter” so that users can choose whether to export all PDFs or only export PDFs for a specific company code.

Any tips or guidance would be highly appreciated! Thank you in advance.

SAP Analytics Cloud

entry.title: Terraform in Practice: Setting Up Joule Studio
entry.content:
Introduction 

As Developer Advocates, we run SAP CodeJam events where participants need hands-on access to the Joule Studio environment, typically provided by us during the day of the event. The challenge: a Joule Studio tenant has limits on the number of deployments that can be made, and these limits are reached in a CodeJam setting where 30+ developers are simultaneously testing their skills and agents, pushing deployments etc, so each CodeJam event requires spinning up a brand new tenant from scratch knowing which subscription, instances, plan, role collections, destinations, and credentials to use while creating a new tenant. 

Doing these tasks by hand is a long and tedious process. It takes too many clicks and careful typing, which makes it easy to make a mistake. With CodeJam events happening frequently, this process was becoming a real bottleneck. Terraform seemed like a good choice at this point to perform these steps. Instead of repeating the same manual steps every time, we could automate the entire tenant provisioning process, turning what used to take significant manual effort into a few commands and getting a Joule Studio tenant at the end of the execution which typically takes 20 to 30 mins. 

I had the opportunity to explore Terraform during a hands-on session at SAP Inside Track, Bangalore in June 2025.  After trying it out in the developer hands on area, I could see its value for automating the creation and management of SAP BTP subaccounts. During Devtoberfest 2025, SAP experts on Terraform presented a session titled From Clicks to Code: Automate SAP BTP Setup with Terraform on how to set up a BTP environment with Terraform. While trying to learn more about Terraform, I also came across a learning.sap.com course that is comphrensive. The course is available here and the GitHub repository used in this learning course is available here. There are also some excellent posts in the SAP Community portal for beginners looking to get started with Terraform, the links to those posts can be found in the Additional References section. Rather than repeating what's already well-documented, I'll focus on the key concepts behind the Terraform and share a few things I picked up along the way and provide you a Terraform script to get started with Joule Studio setup.  

What is Terraform? 

Terraform uses HashiCorp Configuration Language for building infrastructure with configuration files. Terraform's configuration language is declarative, meaning that it describes the desired end state for your infrastructure. It also keeps track of your real infrastructure in a state file, which acts as a source of truth for your environment. Terraform uses the state file to determine the changes to make to your infrastructure so that it will match your configuration. Terraform builds a resource graph to determine resource dependencies and creates or modifies non-dependent resources in parallel. This allows Terraform to provision resources efficiently. Terraform plugins called providers lets Terraform interact with cloud platforms and other services via their application programming interfaces (APIs). SAP provides dedicated Terraform providers for BTP, Cloud Identity, Cloud Foundry, and Cloud Connector. The BTP provider functions by wrapping BTP CLI commands, allowing you to convert imperative CLI commands into declarative Terraform resources. See the registry for documentation on how to use them: https://registry.terraform.io/search/providers?namespace=SAP. 

Using Terraform as an Administrator 

There are two ways to run the terraform script to create a Joule Studio environment. We will look at how to run the Terraform scripts on a local machine with Terraform CLI first. The approach mentioned below requires minor changes depending on whether we are running it on a Mac or Windows machine. 

  1. Clone this repository https://github.com/SAP-samples/joule-studio-setup-terraform
  2. Open it in VS Code
  3. Go through the pre-requisites section in the README file 
  4. Install Terraform CLI https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/tutorials/aws-get-started/install-cli 
  5. Set up environment variables by running these commands on your VS Code terminal  

On Mac/Linux 

export TF_VAR_btp_username='your-btp-username' 
export TF_VAR_btp_password='your-btp-user-password'

On Windows (PowerShell) 

$env:TF_VAR_btp_username = 'your-btp-username' 
$env:TF_VAR_btp_password = 'your-btp-user-password'

6. Configure the values for global account subdomain, identity provider domain, subdomain name and region in the terraform.tfvars file

Project in VS Code 7. Go to the root directory and run the commands from your terminal one by one. 

terraform init
terraform apply --auto-approve

8. Once all the resources are created, run Joule booster to create formation to complete the Joule studio setup 

The terraform init command installs the SAP BTP and Cloud foundry providers configured in the main.tf file in the repository.  

The terraform apply command installs the entitlements and subscriptions mentioned in the .tf files. Refer to GitHub Actions section for the second approach. 

GitHub Actions 

Now, the local Terraform CLI approach works perfectly fine and well suited for a development workflow, but what if you would rather not install anything on your machine at all? 

That's where GitHub Actions comes in. GitHub Actions lets you automate anything, triggered either by an event in your repository (like a push or a PR) or kicked off manually straight from the GitHub UI. Your workflows live as YAML files inside the .github/workflows directory of your repository. 

Check out the README of this repository to follow this approach for creating your Joule Studio setup: https://github.com/SAP-samples/joule-studio-setup-terraform 

One thing worth knowing: you can override any terraform.tfvars variables defined in your repository with the corresponding values in the GitHub variables by passing them via the --var flag. You can see how to pass the variables in the terraform.yml file under .github/workflows of the repository. Anything you haven't explicitly overridden will simply fall back to whatever is defined in terraform.tfvars. 

Use this approach when you have a stable version of a script that can be used to create a new tenant with all the resources and dependencies. 

Using Terraform as a Developer 

My recommendation would be to stick to the local Terraform CLI approach while you are actively writing or updating your scripts. The reason is simple: when something fails during development, Terraform lets you fix just the broken parts and apply only those changes. That kind of control is needed when you are iterating quickly. 

Before you start customizing the script to fit your needs, there are also a handful of expressions and constructs worth getting accustomed to.  So, let’s walk through the significant ones. 

Variables 

All the variables used in the script are defined in the variables.tf file with an optional default value if needed. Configurable values such as global account name, sub account region etc can be provided in the terraform.tfvars file. Sensitive information like passwords and secrets should be stored as environment variables in local machines or as GitHub secrets in GitHub. Terraform makes this easy with the TF_VAR_ prefix. Any environment variable with that prefix gets automatically picked up as a Terraform input variable. So, if you set TF_VAR_btp_password in your environment, Terraform maps it directly to var.btp_password in your script. This keeps credentials in GitHub Secrets or Environment variables and passes them securely to Terraform without exposing them in logs or files. 

If a variable has no default value and you haven't provided it through .tfvars or an environment variable, Terraform will prompt you for it in the terminal during plan or apply. It won't silently fail, but it will interrupt your flow, so it's worth getting your variables sorted before you run anything.  

Built -in functions: 

There is a set of built-in functions available that you can use within your scripts from this reference. Some of the functions used in the repository are lower(), replace(), trim(), toset(), contains() etc. When a subaccount is created, the name of the subaccount subdomain needs to confirm certain rules defined by BTP. We could use these built-in functions to sanitize the user input provided in the terraform.tfvars file and create a subaccount subdomain that confirms those naming conventions.  

raw_subdomain = lower("${var.subaccount_name}-${var.subaccount_subdomain}-${var.subaccount_region}") 
subaccount_subdomain = trim (replace (replace (raw_subdomain, "/[^a-z0-9-]/", "-"), "/--+/", "-"), "-")

This code sanitizes and formats a string to create a subdomain by concatenating three variables into lowercase and using regular expressions to replace any invalid special characters or double-hyphens with a single dash.

Meta-argument 

depends_on is a meta-argument to define explicit dependency. For eg: if you want to create a subscription for Build Work Zone but the entitlement for Build Work Zone should exist before the subscription happens. In such cases, the depends_on argument can be used to ensure that the entitlement is created before the subscription. By default, Terraform executes resources in parallel.

local values block  

When you want to define a local variable or derive a value from a variable, you can use locals. For instance, the cf_api_url is derived from subaccount_region variable, so we can define it within local value block and then use them by referencing it as local.cf_api.url  

locals {
  cf_api_url = https://api.cf.${var.subaccount_region}.hana.ondemand.com 
}

Ternary operators 

Ternary operators can be used to apply conditional expressions. 

plan_name = var.environment == "prod" ? "standard" : "free"

If the environment is production, it assigns the value "standard" to plan_name.

What I learned about the Development workflow 

 After working with Terraform, here is what I consider to be the best practices to follow: 

  • Always start with terraform fmt -recursive before committing anything. It ensures your code style remains consistent and identifies basic syntax issues early, preventing them from causing problems later
  • Run terraform validate after every code change. It's quick, doesn't need credentials, and gives you instant feedback on syntax and data type errors 
  • Never skip the terraform plan -out=tfplan even for "small" changes. This step shows you exactly what Terraform is about to create, modify, or destroy. Review the output carefully before moving on
  • Then apply with terraform apply tfplan using the saved plan file. This guarantees you are applying exactly what you reviewed 
  • Avoid --auto-approve flags in production
  • Never commit .tfstate files as state files can contain sensitive data like passwords and secrets. Add this file to .gitignore. Use a secure remote state backend instead 
  • Always commit .terraform.lock.hcl. This locks your provider versions and ensures everyone on the team gets reproducible results

Further extension of the script 

Think of the script as your starting point, it gets you a working Joule Studio setup quickly. Depending on your scenario, you will likely want to extend it.  

For Joule to connect to SAP AI Core (for document grounding), an S3 bucket (for storing documents), an MCP Server (for access to live data) or external OData APIs via BTP destinations, you will need to add those destinations in Terraform. Create a new destination.tf file, add your destination configurations and commit it to the repository and execute the terraform cli commands.

One other thing worth mentioning is Cloud Identity Services. The script assumes your global account already has Cloud Identity Services set up, which is the case for most existing BTP accounts. If that is your case, all you need to do is provide the identity provider domain value in terraform.tfvars and you are good to go. 

However, if your account doesn't have Cloud Identity Services configured yet in the global account, you will need to add a resource for the entitlement and subscription as well. For that, I would point you to the Cloud Identity Services provider documentation, it covers exactly what you need to set that up. 

 Refer to the samples shown here for setting up destination and Cloud Identity Services

  •  https://registry.terraform.io/providers/SAP/btp/latest/docs/resources/subaccount_destination 
  • https://registry.terraform.io/providers/SAP/sap-cloud-identity-services/latest/docs 

 Additional References

  • https://github.com/SAP-samples/btp-terraform-samples 
  • https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blog-posts-by-members/automating-sap-btp-setup-with-the-new-terraform-provider-for-sap-btp/ba-p/13549469 
  • https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blog-posts-by-sap/extending-infrastructure-as-code-for-sap-btp-managing-resources-on-sap-btp/ba-p/14062455 
  • https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blog-posts-by-members/easy-way-to-automate-and-configure-the-setup-of-a-btp-account-and-cloud/ba-p/13677419 
  • https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blog-posts-by-sap/extending-infrastructure-as-code-for-sap-btp-managing-resources-on-sap-btp/ba-p/14062455

entry.title: ‌🇧🇷‌ Tax Summit 2026
entry.content:
Tax Summit 2026 brings together tax leaders and specialists from across Brazil for two days of focused discussions on the future of taxation. The event provides a forum to examine regulatory change, explore emerging practices, and discuss how organizations can respond to a rapidly evolving tax environment.

Brazil’s tax reform introduces fundamental changes to tax calculation and control. These changes demand tighter integration with ERP systems and a higher standard of data quality. Approaches that rely primarily on downstream reporting are increasingly exposed to inconsistencies, manual rework, and governance challenges.

Featured session: April 1, 12:20-12:50 pm
Tax Reform in practice: How SAP Document and Reporting Compliance and ophera.fiscal are redefining tax compliance in Brazil

This session looks at how an integrated approach to tax compliance helps organizations adapt to the new regulatory model. The speakers will explain how SAP Document and Reporting Compliance (DRC), in combination with ophera.fiscal, enables closer integration with ERP processes, supports assisted tax calculation, and strengthens governance of tax data.

This session looks at how an integrated approach to tax compliance helps organizations adapt to the new regulatory model. The speakers will explain how DRC, in combination with ophera.fiscal, enables closer integration with ERP processes, supports assisted tax calculation, and strengthens governance of tax data.

Speakers

Eduardo Kyono, Product Owner, SAP Document and Reporting Compliance, SAP
João Malfará, SAP Document and Reporting Compliance Consultant, Phoron do Brasil 

The event will be held in Portuguese.

Check out the agenda and register HERE.

Are you interested in learning more about Tax Reform and SAP Document and Reporting Compliance from SAP experts? Join the following Work Zone groups to stay up to date:
  • Brazil - Tax Reform Localization
  • Brazil - SAP Document and Reporting Compliance (EFD-REINF)
  • Brazil - SAP Document and Reporting Compliance (NF-e)

entry.title: BDC : BW Modernization - Key to Innovation for SAP Customers
entry.content:
Introduction :

BW Modernization (SAP BW Modernization) is the process of upgrading and transforming your existing SAP BW system into a modern, flexible, cloud-ready analytics platform.

It Means :

  • Moving from old SAP BW systems (BW 3.x, 7.x) to modern platforms like:
  • BW/4HANA
  • SAP Business Data Cloud (BDC)
  • SAP Datasphere
  • Shifting from traditional data warehousing to real-time, cloud-based analytics

 

Modernize : 

Why Customer Feels BW Modernization is Difficult ?

  • Manual code remediation (ABAP, transformations)
  • Dependency mapping is complex
  • Risk of breaking existing reports
  • Time-consuming (months/years)
  • Lack of skilled BW resources

Customer Feels :
“Transformation is not just technical — it’s risky and slow.”

  • Customers fear modernization because of risk
  • Too many objects → Info Cubes, DSO, queries
  • This is where AI changes the gam

Best Approach for BW Modernization :

Lift :

Shift: 

Innovate : 

How AI-Assists 

Business Value to our Customers :

  • 50–70% faster modernization (with AI)
  • Reduced cost & effort
  • Real-time insights
  • AI-driven decision-making
  • Future-ready architecture

 “Modernizing BW is no longer a project — it’s an intelligent transformation.”

Outcome :

  • Focus on value, not technology
  • Faster ROI
  • Better decisions
  • Competitive advantage

 

Thank you!

Regards,

Giri

entry.title: Re: Implementing Custom Logic to Include Project Informat...
entry.content: Hi Chris, Thank you for your quick response. For your information, I have already reviewed the provided links and implemented the solution as described. Based on the Business Scenario | SAP Help Portal, I followed the below steps: Created an extension field for the Billing Document and extended the email template Forwarded the extension field from Sales to Billing Created a Custom CDS View Populated the extension field for the Billing Output Header (via BAdI implementation) Regarding step 4, I created custom logic "YY1_SALESHEADERMODIFICATION" with the following: Affected Business Context: SD_SALESDOC BAdI: Modification of Header Custom Fields in Sales Documents (SD_SLS_MODIFY_HEAD) I also implemented the code as referenced in step 4.6. Additionally, I extended our existing billing document email template (based on the standard template "SD_BD_DEFAULT_EMAIL_TEMPLATE") to include the field: {{YY1_CustomerProjectID_BDH}} Testing Performed Created a sales order and generated a billing document Triggered the email output Issue Observed The email output still shows: "000000000000000000000000" instead of the expected value. I further analyzed the issue and modified the logic to read data from I_SalesDocumentItem. When tested within the Custom Logic app, it returned the correct values. However, the email output continues to display the same incorrect value. Current Understanding It appears that the custom logic implemented under SD_SALESDOC is not being triggered during the email generation process. From the documentation of the affected business context, it states that this BAdI is used for modifying sales document header extension fields, but it does not explicitly confirm its usage for email template extensions. In contrast, for print forms, the documentation for: Header Modification in Standard Output Form for Billing Documents (SD_BIL_PRINT_STANDARD_HEAD) clearly specifies that custom fields for form templates should be handled there. I also reviewed the following documentation: https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/a376cd9ea00d476b96f18dea1247e6a5/0b6ec00b397349b09ec69ec7e60c2667.html?locale=en-US This explains how to update customer invoices but does not clearly indicate where to implement logic specifically for billing email template extensions. Request Could you please advise on the correct extension point or BAdI that should be used to populate custom fields in the billing email template? This field is critical for our business, as the Project ID / Project Description must be included in the invoice email sent to customers. If needed, I can share detailed screenshots and documentation of the development and testing performed. Please let me know the best way to provide these. Thanks in advance for your guidance.

entry.title: Beyond Row-Level Security: Implementing Widget-Level Custom Authorization in SAP Analytics Cloud
entry.content:
The Challenge: One Dashboard to Rule Them All (But Not Show Them Everything)

We’ve all been there. The Board of Directors requests a "Management Dashboard". They want a 360-degree view of the company: Production efficiency, HR turnover, Finance liquidity, and Sales KPIs all in one place.

But here is the catch: While the CEO wants to see everything, the Head of Production shouldn't be distracted by sensitive HR Payroll charts, and the Sales Lead doesn't need to see the Finance department's deep-dive debt ratios.

The Problem: Standard Row-Level Security (RLS) only filters the data inside the charts. If a user isn't authorized, they often see a "No Data" ghost-chart or a broken visualization. This ruins the Executive Experience.

The Goal: If you aren't authorized to see the data, the chart shouldn't even exist on your screen, or in other words, it should not render.

In SAC, this is achieved by bridging the gap between the User Profile and the Canvas Scripting Engine. We aren't just securing data; we are orchestrating the user interface.

Prerequisites for Success

Before we dive into the "How-To," ensure you have the following in place:

  1. User Mapping Table: A central source (could be an acquired CSV or an import data from any system) that maps User IDs to their respective Functional Areas/KPIs.

  2. Consistent Naming Conventions: Ensure your charts are named logically in the Outline (e.g., Chart_Inv_Units, Chart_Fin_Revenue, Chart_Units_Sold) to make your scripts maintainable.
  3. Dashboard with KPIs visualized: Have a dashboard ready with some charts in it. (In this example., Revenue, Units Sold, and Units in the Inventory are the numeric KPI tiles.)

The image shows you the charts in the dashboard with their respective names.

Note: Having all the Charts/Widgets in a flow layout panel helps the flexibility of adjusting the position of the widgets based on the visibility of other widgets.

The Solution: Step-by-Step Implementation

1. Defining the Authorization Matrix

Start by creating a specialized model that defines who sees what. This acts as our "Source of Truth" for the UI. 

In this use case, I use Excel as an authorization matrix.

user_idkpi_tiledummy
GE285115rev1
GE283973uni1
GE283973invuni1
GE285115uni1

From the table, we get to know that the user GE285115 can see only "rev" and "uni" kpis, but not the "invuni". And the user GE283973 can see "uni" and "invuni" kpis, but not the "rev" kpi.

Now, using this table from Excel or from any system, create an import model that looks exactly like this.

2. The onInitialization Scripting Magic

The heart of this solution lies in the onInitialization event. When the dashboard loads, we don't wait for the user to click anything. We programmatically "audit" the user and hide unauthorized components.

Before implementing the code in the onInitialization, create a table using the Auth model that was created in the previous step with the following fields in it, and make it hidden by default.

Here's the code for our example:

Application.showBusyIndicator("Applying Visual level authorization...");
var user_id = Application.getUserInfo();
var i = 0;
Table_1.getDataSource().setDimensionFilter("user_id",user_id.id);
var result = Table_1.getDataSource().getResultSet();
console.log(result);
for(i=0;i<result.length;i++){
	if(result[i]["kpi_tile"].id==="rev")
	{
		Chart_Fin_Revenue.setVisible(true);
	}
	else if(result[i]["kpi_tile"].id==="uni")
	{
		Chart_Units_Sold.setVisible(true);
	}
	else if(result[i]["kpi_tile"].id==="invuni")
	{
		Chart_Inv_Units.setVisible(true);
	} // Add else if incase if you have additional kpis
}
Application.hideBusyIndicator();

And make the default visibility of each chart hidden, as shown below.

3. Testing

Logged in as GE283973 (Assumption: This user should see only "Units Sold" and "Units in Inventory" KPI)

Logged in as GE285115 (Assumption: This user should see only "Units Sold" and "Revenue" KPI)

Lifecycle & Governance:

Maintaining your security matrix shouldn’t be a headache. Whether you’re uploading a flat file or scheduling a job from a source system to the auth model, the admin or the model owner has to maintain it. Always set your import method to "Clean and replace selected version of data." This ensures that as your organizational structure evolves, your dashboard security reflects the current reality, not yesterday’s permissions.

Why this matters for the Enterprise

By implementing Visualisation Level Security, you provide:

  • Reduced Cognitive Load: Executives only see what is actionable for them.
  • Clean Authorization: Instead of showing 'No Data' errors or broken charts, the UI dynamically hides unauthorized KPIs, maintaining a professional and secure dashboard layout.
  • Scalability: You maintain one dashboard instead of five different versions for five different departments.

Pro tip: One can pause the refresh for the widgets that are invisible to the users to enhance the dashboard's performance.

I hope this guide helps you elevate your next SAC project from a standard report to a premium executive experience. Have you run into similar challenges with UI-level security? If you have questions or hit a snag during your implementation, feel free to drop a comment below. I’d love to troubleshoot with you and hear your thoughts!

Happy Learning!!!

Vijay Bhaskaran

entry.title: Sending Dynamic Slack Notifications from SAP Cloud Integration (CPI) Using Groovy Script
entry.content:
Introduction

In modern integration scenarios, real-time notifications play a crucial role in keeping teams informed about system events such as deployments, alerts, or failures.

One effective way to achieve this is by integrating SAP Cloud Integration (CPI) with collaboration tools like Slack.

In this blog, we will build an iFlow that dynamically sends Slack notifications using Groovy scripts, based on user-specific data received in a JSON payload.

Use Case / Scenario

Consider a scenario where:

  • A system sends a JSON payload containing multiple users
  • Each user needs to receive a personalized Slack notification
  • The CPI iFlow processes this payload and sends messages individually

Sample JSON Payload

iFlow Design Overview

The integration flow consists of the following steps:

  • HTTPS Sender Adapter (triggered via Postman)
  • Groovy Script (Extract common properties)
  • JSON to XML Converter
  • General Splitter (split users)
  • Groovy Script (build Slack message)
  • Open Connectors Adapter (send to Slack)

Step 1: Extract Properties Using Groovy Script

  • This script extracts common fields like priority and reference and stores them as message properties.

Step 2: JSON to XML Conversion

  • The JSON payload is converted into XML format
  • This enables XPath usage in the splitter step

Step 3: General Splitter Configuration

  • The General Splitter splits the payload into individual users
  • Each user is processed separately
  • XPath Example: /users

Step 4: Build Slack Message Using Groovy Script

  • This script dynamically constructs a Slack message for each user.

Step 5: Send Notification to Slack

Use Open Connectors Adapter

Configure Slack connector with:

  • Channel name
  • Authentication
  • Message body

Testing Using Postman

  • Send a POST request to CPI endpoint
  • Use the sample JSON payload
  • Trigger the iFlow
  • Here I used Students details as example JSON payload

Slack Output (Expected Result)

Each user will receive a message like:

 Key Benefits

  • Dynamic message generation
  • Handles multiple users efficiently
  • Real-time Slack notifications
  • Reusable Groovy scripts

Challenges Faced

During implementation, a few challenges were addressed:

  • Handling null values in payload
  • Ensuring proper JSON parsing
  • Formatting Slack messages dynamically
  • Managing splitter configuration correctly

Conclusion

Using SAP Cloud Integration with Groovy scripting, we can easily build scalable and dynamic notification systems.

This approach is highly useful for:

  • Deployment notifications
  • Alerting systems
  • Monitoring integrations

It enhances visibility and ensures teams stay updated in real time.

#SAPCPI #SAPIntegrationSuite #Groovy #SlackIntegration #Automation

 @Souravraj_Maitra

@Bibekananda1231

entry.title: Re: Maintain PIR app and splitting indicator in S4 Hana p...
entry.content:
Good day @srinivasareddybhimanadham 

Thank you for your question.

Question 1: How to load the forecast on weekly basis ? excel upload we have option for only monthly.

Answer: Based on my understanding of what you intend to achieve, which is not use Excel because it allows you to only upload monthly forecast. Therefore, my suggestion would be for you to use weekly PIRs directly, it requires the material to have Weekly as forecast period indicator. In that case, Maintain PIRs supports weekly maintenance with 52 weekly periods.

See the following references for more insights.

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/2bba750d1e124e1ea2a039bb1cd9b6c5/03c2dd11c91f4df18e65ea1886dd54e0.html?locale=en-US

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/2bba750d1e124e1ea2a039bb1cd9b6c5/71b373b9418746c09a9e8bf0123cc958.html?locale=en-US

 

Question 2: unable to find Automatic splitting indicator of monthly loaded forecast into weekly, how to achieve this in S4 public cloud, any workaround solution for this?

Answer: You are trying to carry out a Public Cloud equivalent of the old plant level splitting indicator customising from R/3.  The practical workaround in Public Cloud is either: Direct weekly PIR maintenance or SAP IBP based weekly integration back to S/4HANA cloud.

So, yes, a workaround solution would be:

  • For you to set the material for weekly PIR planning and maintain weekly values directly
  • Or you use SAP IBP to generate and load weekly PIRs
  • If the forecast is still monthly, either split the file outside S/4 before you upload it or use SAP IBP to forecast and return weekly PIRs back to the Public Cloud.

Here are references for more guidance

2748331 - Maintain PIRs app doesn't show weekly/monthly PIRs - SAP for Me

3504764 - Splitting the monthly forecast data into equal quantities from Monday to Friday. - SAP for Me

3536168 - "Days" period indicator is not available in Maintain PIRs app - SAP for Me

 

Let me know if you have further questions.

Best regards

Chris

entry.title: K6 Load Testing for SAP BTP (CAP + Fiori): Scope, CI/CD, and User Simulation?
entry.content:
Hi everyone, 

I’m currently getting familiar with testing in the SAP BTP ecosystem, since I'm working on a MTA app deployed on the Build Work Zone. I've started working with Wdi5 for the front and middle, and it seems K6 is the best option for load testing. 

Most K6 resources are generic, and I’m unsure how well they translate to SAP-specific architectures (XSUAA, destinations, OData, etc.). I have a few questions, which answers that would help me a great deal. 

1) What can reasonnably be tested with K6?

Should load testing target OData/CAP services directly or is there value in simulating flows through the Build Work Zone (E2E)? I'm assuming for now that backend-focused testing is more relevant, but maybe I’m missing some SAP-specific considerations.

2) Can K6 be integrated to a CI/CD pipeline in the same ways that Wdi5 can?

I’m using Azure DevOps with a standard MTA build/deploy pipeline. Should K6 tests run after deployment to a test environment, and are there common patterns for .yml configuration in this context?

3) What load models make the most sense?

K6 supports both Virtual Users and Requests per second. How can virtual users be tested? And should they even be tested?

In other word, should I simulate concurrent users or throughput against services? I suspect throughput-based testing may be more realistic, but I’d like confirmation.

4) User simulation?

Lastly - and this is the least clear part: how (and should I) mock users? If yes, what is the best method to do so? I'm thinking reusing a single OAuth token, since separate XSUAA auth seems very impractical.

Thanks for your guidance, and really looking forward any insight you might have!

Best, 

Theodore

entry.title: Enhancing Custom PS Report: Handling 1:N Relationship between WBS Element and Sales Orders (VBAP)
entry.content:
Hello
I am working on an enhancement for a custom Project System report (similar to a project overview/status report).

Current Situation: The report currently displays project details based on a WBS Element. However, the logic for fetching associated Sales Orders is currently limited to a 1:1 relationship. 

The Requirement: Our Project Managers often have multiple Sales Orders assigned to a single WBS element (e.g., one for services, one for materials, and one for an add-on).

  • Example Data in VBAP:

    • WBS: P.*******.1.3 -> SO: 113984

    • WBS: P.*******.1.3 -> SO: 113989

    • WBS: P.*******.1.3 -> SO: 113992

Currently, the report only reflects the first Sales Order. The goal is to update the report logic to display all associated Sales Orders for that single WBS number to provide a comprehensive revenue view.

Technical Questions:

  1. What is the best practice for displaying these multiple rows in an ALV-based report? Should I duplicate the WBS line for each SO, or use a "merged cells" approach in the layout?

  2. Are there any specific performance considerations when fetching all items from Table: VBAP for a large list of WBS elements?

  3. Besides VBAP , is there a more efficient standard SAP view or secondary index I should consider to find Sales Orders linked to a WBS?

Environment: SAP S/4HANA 

Any advice on the logic flow or standard Function Modules that handle this PS-SD link would be greatly appreciated!

entry.title: Re: Increment counter function in sap build process autom...
entry.content:
Hello @liskaj01 ,

The initialize counter and increment counter functions aren’t suitable for your requirement.

  • Recommended approach: If you need to count loop iterations, use a forever or foreach loop—these provide an index value starting from 0, which you can use for counting.

  • Alternatively, you can create a number variable and increment its value by 1 within the loop to achieve the same result.

entry.title: Inquiry: Does SAP Build Work Zone Support CALM-Based Transport Configuration
entry.content:
Hello SAP Support Team,

I would like to seek clarification regarding transport management integration for SAP Build Work Zone.

Based on available documentation, SAP Build Work Zone currently supports transport movement primarily through SAP Cloud Transport Management Service (cTMS), which can optionally integrate with SAP Cloud ALM for deployment workflows. Multiple SAP Community and official SAP Help sources confirm the following:

  • SAP Build Work Zone objects (roles, catalogs, apps, sites, etc.) can be transported using cTMS, configured via service instances, destinations, and transport nodes in BTP subaccounts. 
  • cTMS integration can be linked with SAP Cloud ALM for deployment and transport orchestration, leveraging ALM for change tracking and landscape flow. [ainvest.com], [community.sap.com]
  • SAP Cloud ALM provides its own transport management support (for S/4HANA and other systems), but the documentation does not expli

entry.title: Re: Unable to set property from an XML using the XPath.
entry.content:
The entry is in a namespace, but the XPath prefix wasn't assigned.

XPath in CPI does not select default namespaces by default.

Prefixes must be defined in CPI (in Content Modifier → XPath):

Namespace mapping:
atom → http://www.w3.org/2005/Atom 
d → http://schemas.microsoft.com/ado/2007/08/dataservices


The right XPath
/atom:entry/d:fileExtension/text()

Please try this way.

Thanks,

Akhilesh

entry.title: Re: French e-invoicing & S/4HANA Cloud: partial vs residu...
entry.content:
Hi Corinne,

Payment are either reported as CDAR 212 status for e-Invoicing or as flow 10.2/10.4 (B2B International/B2C) for e-Reporting when it is related to service (except for Incoming Down Payment).

As such, it is usually related to Company submitted to Deferred VAT. Even long before the upcoming French e-invoicing and e-reporting reform, SAP always recommended to use Partial Payment in this case to have an accurate transfer of Deferred VAT to VAT On Payment (the residual item would clear the full Open Item and recreate one, leading to incorrect VAT amount).

It means that the CDAR will be created for the amount partially paid (e-Invoicing). About e-Reporting, it is also the partial incoming payment that will be send.

The way you manage partial payment does not change in SAP with the upcoming e-Invoicing/e-Reporting. You will keep posting your document the same way and thus, no specific guidance is needed here.

Regards

Alexandre

SAP Document and Reporting Compliance #France #eInvoicing

entry.title: Re: SuccessFactors Compensation - Move employee's compens...
entry.content:
Did you check this box in the update; Apply the above selected updates to completed compensation worksheets as well. By default, update only applies to in-progress worksheets.? The completed forms would not appear on the salary tab of the current manager because they were not part of the workflow and therefore were never owners of the form. They would not see them in the completed folder.. Executive Review is based on RBP and should allow the new manager to see the records in their current state. I would consult the client as to why they cannot use executive review. As to why some show and some don't, there are a bunch of reasons why this would occur based on data and timing of transactions/updates. You would have to investigate each scenario further.

It seems like the system is working as designed but there are a lot of things happening in your scenario.

entry.title: Re: User not able to share bookmarks in sac stories
entry.content:
Dear saurabhverma,

Hope you are well. Thank you for your message.

For this scenario, it is also worth checking whether this user is able to share other objects, such as story or model, with other users in this SAC system. In "Share Model" or "Share Story" dialog, whether the user list can appear as expected?

If the user list does not appear as expected in any Share dialog, it is recommend reviewing the help document Privileges and Permissions | SAP Help Portal.. This document provides detailed guidance on permissions required to view user lists in such dialogs.

As outlined in the document, the Read permission for the User object is essential to access and display the list of users. This permission allows you to see users in various scenarios, such as when selecting individuals to share a story with or adding members to a team.

To ensure the user has the appropriate permissions, it is therefore recommended to verify whether this user, who shares the object in question, has been granted at least "Read" access to the "User" object. This can be checked within the settings of the role assigned to the user. 

Thank you for your time.

Best wishes,

Lena

entry.title: Smaller ABAP Runtime Size Now Available in SAP BTP ABAP Environment
entry.content:
Starting March 31, 2026, SAP BTP ABAP environment introduces a new runtime sizing option: 0.5 ABAP Compute Units (ACU) which is 8 GB of runtime memory.

With the introduction of 0.5 ACU, customers can now operate ABAP systems at 50% lower ABAP runtime cost compared to the previous minimum size of 1 ACU making it significantly more cost-efficient to run development, test, and sandbox systems.

This enhancement allows SAP BTP ABAP environment to run on smaller runtime units,  lowering the entry barrier for development and test systems.

What is an ABAP Compute Unit (ACU)?

ABAP Compute Units (ACUs) define the runtime capacity of an SAP BTP ABAP environment system. One ACU corresponds to 16 GB of memory allocated to the ABAP runtime. The total runtime size determines how much capacity is available across all ABAP application servers. For more information, see the documentation on ABAP Compute Units.

What’s New?

With this update SAP BTP ABAP environment now supports 0.5 ABAP Compute Units (ACU) as the new minimum system configuration.

It allows to run smaller systems, enabling more granular runtime sizing compared to previous configurations. It creates an additional option to better match the actual workloads, particularly for smaller environments.

What are the Key Benefits?

  • 50% lower ABAP runtime cost compared to the previous minimum size (1 ACU)
  • Improved resource utilization for smaller or variable workloads
  • Lower entry barrier for new and existing customers

For a detailed breakdown of cost components, see my previous blog post on ABAP environment cost calculation.

How to Adjust Existing Systems?

Adjusting the ABAP runtime size (ACUs) is handled via manual scaling and does not lead to a system downtime . You can use the SAP BTP Cockpit to update the service instance, for example, from 1 ACU to 0,5 ACU, see the documentation on Updating an ABAP System.

What Needs to be Considered?

For systems with a runtime size of 0.5 ACU, only one application server is used. This setup is ideal for development and test, but not recommended for productive use, as it may increase the likelihood of short outages.

Overview about typical use cases and recommend systems sizes

Use Case

Size

Total Runtime MemoryApplication Servers

Development, test, sandbox systems for smaller projects

0.5 ACU (new)

8 GB1 á 8 GB
Development, test, sandbox systems for bigger projects;
Productive systems for up to 1,000 active users per day		1 ACU16 GB2 á 8 GB
Productive systems with higher concurrency or up to 100 tenants 2 ACU32 GB4 á 8 GB
Productive systems with bigger max session size4 ACU64 GB2 á 32 GB
Productive systems with higher load in multitenant SaaS solution 4 ACU64 GB8 á 8 GB

 

Further References 

For more information about sizing options and configuration parameters in SAP BTP ABAP environment, see the documentation on Creating an ABAP System.

entry.title: Critical error in @sap/cds-dk + @sap/hdi-deploy (handlebars)
entry.content:
Hello,

We cannot deploy our application to our environments because the npm dependency audit is throwing a critical warning:
handlebars  4.0.0 - 4.7.8
Severity: critical
Handlebars.js has JavaScript Injection in CLI Precompiler via Unescaped Names and Options - https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-xjpj-3mr7-gcpf
Handlebars.js has JavaScript Injection via AST Type Confusion when passing an object as dynamic partial - https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-xhpv-hc6g-r9c6
Handlebars.js has Denial of Service via Malformed Decorator Syntax in Template Compilation - https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-9cx6-37pm-9jff
Handlebars.js has JavaScript Injection via AST Type Confusion by tampering @partial-block - https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-3mfm-83xf-c92r
Handlebars.js has JavaScript Injection via AST Type Confusion - https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-2w6w-674q-4c4q
Handlebars.js has Prototype Pollution Leading to XSS through Partial Template Injection - https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-2qvq-rjwj-gvw9
Handlebars.js has a Prototype Method Access Control Gap via Missing __lookupSetter__ Blocklist Entry - https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-7rx3-28cr-v5wh
Handlebars.js has a Property Access Validation Bypass in container.lookup - https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-442j-39wm-28r2
fix available via `npm audit fix`
node_modules/@sap/cds-dk/node_modules/handlebars


The problem seems to be that the latest version in the 8.9.x range (@sap/cds-dk 8.9.15) which we are using, has @sap/hdi-deploy 5.6.1 as dependency. This version of @sap/hdi-deploy has the package handlebars as a dependency, which was updated recently to version 4.7.9.
Please update the @sap/hdi-deploy package so that it uses handlebars 4.7.9, and update @sap/cds-dk to a new version that uses the updated @sap/hdi-deploy as dependency
