------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: TDS Adjustment Entry and impact on the TDS report
entry.content:
Hi all,

 

Please note that if TDS has been deducted incorrectly or if there is a need to change the applicable tax rate, the recommended approach is to reverse the original document and repost it with the correct details.

Alternatively, adjust the TDS amount by passing a rectification entry through the ''Enter Journal Voucher'' application, where applicable.

The above approaches ensure proper correction of TDS values while maintaining accurate accounting records and audit compliance. It is advisable to review the impact on related documents (such as invoices or payments) before performing any reversal or adjustment.

 

Best regards,

Atanu

SAP Support - S/4HANA Cloud

entry.title: SAP Document and Reporting Compliance UAE: Electronic Invoice in Utilities using Extensibility
entry.content:
You want to enable your system to create electronic documents for invoices posted in Utilities using SAP Document and Reporting Compliance. In this blog, we’ll show you an example on how this can be achieved using extensibility.  

Before you begin ensure that you have implemented the SAP Notes listed in 3705171 - Electronic Document Processing for United Arab Emirates - Peppol: Implementation Overview. You can also refer the SAP Help Portal for more information, see Electronic Document Processing.
Ensure you have also implemented the SAP Notes listed in 2736880 - eDocument EU PEPPOL: Overview note for IS-U.

Steps to Create Electronic Documents

  • Create eDocument Class
    1. Create 'ZCL_EDOCUMENT_AE_ISU' eDocument class by inheriting CL_EDOCUMENT_EU_ISU class.
    2. Add the CONSTRUCTOR method in the ZCL_EDOCUMENT_AE_ISU eDocument class to map the 'Z' eDocument IS-U class name to the field ms_edocument-edocument_class. 
    3. Redefine the method DETERMINE_PROCESS to assign the processes AEINV and AEINVIN.
    4. Use the table TFK003EDOC to configure eDocument type against the accounting document type.
    Example:

    Country/Region 

    Application Area 

    Document Type 

    eDocument Type 

    IN 

    Utility Company 

    FA 

    AE_INV 

     5. Add the class CL_EDOC_PROCESS as Friends of 'ZCL_EDOCUMENT_AE_ISU' eDocument class. 

    Sample Code for CONSTRUCTOR and DETERMINE_PROCESS method:  
    Constructor method signature: 

     


METHOD constructor.
 super->constructor( io_source_data                 = io_source_data
                      iv_edoc_guid                   = iv_edoc_guid
                      iv_land                        = iv_land
                      iv_update_task                 = iv_update_task
                      iv_generic_badi_filter_adaptor = iv_generic_badi_filter_adaptor
                      iv_simulate_for_email_option = iv_simulate_for_email_option ).

  ms_edocument-land                   = 'AE'.
  ms_edocument-edocument_class        = 'ZCL_EDOCUMENT_AE_ISU'.
ENDMETHOD.
METHOD determine_process.
    CASE ms_edocument-edoc_type.
      WHEN 'AE_INVIN'.
        ms_edocument-process = 'AEINVIN'.
        ms_edocument-process_version = '0001'.
      WHEN OTHERS.
        CASE ms_edocument-interface_type.
          WHEN 'AIF_PROXY' OR 'STANDARD'.
            ms_edocument-process = 'AEINV'.
*           reference process is 'EUINV'
            ms_edocument-process_version = '0001'.
          WHEN OTHERS.
            MESSAGE e162(edocument) INTO gv_error_txt.
            raise_edoc_exception( ).
        ENDCASE.
    ENDCASE.
ENDMETHOD.

 

  • Create Factory Class
    1. Create a 'Z' factory class to get the instance of eDocument IS-U class by inheriting CL_EDOC_FACTORY class and redefine its method CREATE_EDOC_INSTANCE. 
    Example: ZCL_EDOC_FACTORY_AE_ISU
    2. Configure this 'Z' eDocument factory class in the view EDOFACTORYV using the transaction SM30.

    Source Type  

    Factory Class  

    ISU_INVOIC  

    ‘Z’ factory class name (your factory class that you just created)  


    Sample Code:
METHOD create_edoc_instances.

    DATA: lv_land_iso      TYPE edoc_land,
          lv_error_txt     TYPE string,
          ls_source_header TYPE edoc_src_header,
          lo_edocument     TYPE REF TO zcl_edocument_ae_isu.

    CLEAR: lv_land_iso,
           lv_error_txt,
           ls_source_header,
           lo_edocument,
           et_edocument_obj.

* Country
    ls_source_header = io_source->get_header( ).
    CALL FUNCTION 'COUNTRY_CODE_SAP_TO_ISO'
      EXPORTING
        sap_code  = ls_source_header-land
      IMPORTING
        iso_code  = lv_land_iso
      EXCEPTIONS
        not_found = 1
        OTHERS    = 2.
    IF sy-subrc <> 0.
      MESSAGE e881(b1) WITH ls_source_header-land INTO lv_error_txt.
    ENDIF.
    IF lv_land_iso <> 'AE'.
      RETURN.
    ENDIF.

    CREATE OBJECT lo_edocument 
      EXPORTING
        io_source_data = io_source
        iv_update_task = iv_update_task.

    APPEND lo_edocument TO et_edocument_obj.

  ENDMETHOD.

  • Mapping Class
    1. We have a generic mapping class CL_EDOC_MAP_UBL_ISU. If you need to enhance the mapping logic, you can use the EDOC_ADAPTOR BAdI's method SET_OUTPUT_DATA. For more details, refer to Enhancements for Electronic Documents.
    Alternatively, you can implement your own custom mapping class. 
    2. Configure your mapping class in the view EDOMAPCLASSDETV using the transaction SM30. 
    Example:

    eDocument Process 

    Version 

    Interface ID 

    eDocument Type 

    Source Type 

    Mapping Class 

    EUINV 

    1 

    UBL_21_INVOICE_REQUEST

    AE_INV 

    ISU_INVOIC 

    <Your Z mapping class or CL_EDOC_MAP_UBL_ISU> 

    EUINV

    1

    UBL_21_INVOICE_RESPONSE

    AE_INV

     

    CL_EDOC_MAP_UBL

     

  • Maintain the Required Customizations
    Apart from maintaining Master Data, Customizing and Integration as explained in the SAP Help Portal, you must also configure the company code for the following IS-U source type.

    1.  Activate company code for ISU_INVOIC source type in the view EDOCOMPANYACTIV for eDocument creation.
    Example:

    Company Code  

    Source Type  

    Application Active  

    <Your company code>  

    ISU_INVOICE  

    X  


By following these steps, you can efficiently create electronic documents for invoices in the utilities sector, ensuring compliance and enhancing operational efficiency. 

Don't forget to test it thoroughly. 

We hope this blog post was useful for you! You can leave a comment here or continue browsing our community for more blog posts on SAP Document and Reporting Compliance topics. 

Looking forward to seeing you here again!

entry.title: Re: Event Mesh + AMQP: The Retry Trap Nobody Warns You About
entry.content:
@RubikWal 

I really appreciate having someone like you to challenge my perspective from different angles!

DataStore isn't specifically tailored for JMS. It works well alongside AMQP.  AMQP is excellent for the initial call and handling transient errors via automatic retries. However, for more permanent errors - where individual processing (whether automatic, semi-automatic, or manual) is required - I recommend using DataStore as the storage layer for a Dead Letter Queue.

Yesterday another fantastic tool came out for DataStore management, pls have a look:
https://community.sap.com/t5/integration-blog-posts/cloud-integration-pipeline-datastore-extension-ui/ba-p/14326020 

Feel free to send me DM on LinkedIn so we can continue this discussion outside the comments section
BR, Mateusz

entry.title: Re: Best Practice for Intercompany Project Timesheets in...
entry.content:
Good day @SAPPS 

Thank you for your question.

The simple best practice I can recommend is as follows:

  • Keep the executing project in Company B
  • Staff the employee from Company A to the relevant work package in that project
  • Let the employee record time against the staffed assignment using Manage My Timesheet

  • Process the posted time (through Process Unposted Time Confirmations) and, where needed, initiate intercompany billing from the sending company to the receiving company.

 

Regarding your Scenario is: An employee of a Company A wants to book timesheets in a Project getting executed in Company B.

Yes, that is the standard business design, provided the employee is staffed correctly and the intercompany charging design is in place. The whole purpose of resource related intercompany billing is that one company code provides services for another company code.

 

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/29cf986299714386847f4d4c9bb86994/21218854c46ee75ee10000000a44176d.html?locale=en-US

2870580 - Inter Company Time Recording is not allowed for Unstaffed Projects - SAP for Me

 

Let me know if you have further questions.

Best regards

Chris

entry.title: Re: Best Practice for Intercompany Project Timesheets in...
entry.content:
Dear @SAPPS,

Recommended/proposal process steps:

1. The project should be created in the receiving company and moved to In Execution status. SAP documents that once the project is in execution, resources can book time to it via Manage My Timesheet. If stricter control is required, set Restrict Unstaffed Posting = Yes, so only staffed resources can post time to the project

More details below:

https://learning.sap.com/courses/managing-projects-and-resources-in-sap-s-4hana-cloud-public-edition-professional-services/managing-time-recording-and-approvals

2. The employee should be assigned as a project resource, ideally with a hard-booked assignment. SAP documents that a hard booking is the confirmed assignment status and, once hard-booked, the staffing information is updated immediately. SAP also describes intercompany project execution as a process where one company delivers services for another company project and confirms that project management supports staffing resources across companies

More details below:

https://learning.sap.com/courses/managing-projects-and-resources-in-sap-s-4hana-cloud-public-edition-professional-services/analyzing-business-process-steps-and-apps-for-sap-s-4hana-cloud-resource-management

https://learning.sap.com/courses/exploring-sales-intercompany-processes-in-sap-s-4hana-cloud-public-edition-professional-services/understanding-intercompany-billing-for-cross-company-cost-accounting-postings_da2dcbc8-0efa-4042-91ba-b7531f866d2c

3. After staffing, the project appears automatically in the employee My Tasks area in Manage My Timesheet and the employee can post hours directly to that project/work package. SAP further states that once time is booked and approved where applicable, it is reflected on the project as both a time and cost posting

4. If approval is part of the process, submitted time entries are routed to My Inbox for review and approval. SAP documents this as the standard approval mechanism for project-related time entries

More details below for points 3 and 4:

https://learning.sap.com/courses/managing-projects-and-resources-in-sap-s-4hana-cloud-public-edition-professional-services/managing-time-recording-and-approvals

5. For the delivering company, SAP documents a standard flow consisting of:

  • creation of the intercompany sales order as a one-time setup for each delivering company involved
  • execution of Generate Intercompany Billing Request using the job template Resource-Related Billing Between Company Codes
  • creation of a DMR3 debit memo request containing the time recordings and expenses and
  • creation of the final intercompany invoice, after which SAP can automatically create the mirrored accounts payable document in the receiving company

Summary:

In Public Cloud the recommended best practice for this scenario is to keep the employee in Company A as the delivering resource, keep the project in Company B as the receiving company, staff the Company A employee to the Company B project/work package and let the employee record time there using Manage My Timesheet. The resulting cross-company cost posting is then settled through the standard resource-related intercompany billing process. SAP explicitly documents that applications such as time recording for projects or services can create cross-company cost accounting documents and that for these documents the required legal invoicing is supported through the intercompany billing process, with the receiving company payable created automatically

Best Regards,

Dawid

entry.title: Re: sac的年龄计算有问题，目前是出生日期当天年龄就+1，和实际不符合&#...
entry.content:
Hi Jimmy,

Hope you are well. Thank you for your message.

From a standard international age perspective, the calculation is:

  • Birthdate: 2001‑01‑31
  • Reference date: 2026‑01‑31
  • When someone reaches their birthday in a given year, they turn a year older.
  • On 2026‑01‑31, Chen Daxin’s birthday arrives, so he/she turns 25.

Therefore, 25 is mathematically correct for international age (full-year age).

If it is expected age to be "international age-1" when the Month and Day are the same for the Birthday and Reference date, it is required to create an additional calculation in story. For example, the calculation can be defined as below:

IF([d/"Mdoel A":Birthday].[p/MONTHDESC]=[d/"Model A":Reference date].[p/MONTHDESC]and[d/"Model A":Birthday].[p/DAY]=[d/"Model A":Reference date].[p/DAY] ;[#Date Difference ]-1 ; [#Date Difference])

Thank you for your time.

Best wishes,

Lena

entry.title: Re: The Data Ownership dilemma — and how SAP Business Data Cloud Data Products helps resolve It
entry.content:
Thank you for your comments.

Here is what I can tell you :
@Anthony : Data Products are functionnaly quite similar to the BW Business Content..
But what changes is that BW wan only focusing on ERP data while Data Products focus on ALL SAP data : S/4, Concur, SuccessFactors, Ariba ... AND non-SAP data, leveraging technology standards such as SQL language,  spark ...
And, we also deliver out of the box reporting (Intelligent Applications). This was not available in former BWs

@nachtaktiv : this relies on the concept of Clean core. Most of the processes can be standardized, and SAP was implemented to match these standard processes. Data products reuse all the BusinessProcess that is available out of the box within SAP applications. If customers need to modify/adapt these standard process, then indeed, IT will work on the modified processes. But in most cases, 80% remains standard.

entry.title: Asset Accounting
entry.content:
Hi,

I am currently working on a project where SAP ECC 6.0 is already implemented. At present, I am involved in a Chart of Accounts (COA) restructuring activity within the same system, with a focus on Asset Accounting.

The client requirement is to assign new GL accounts to existing assets. To achieve this, I have created new asset classes, configured account determination, and assigned new GL accounts in AO90.

Now, the client expects that the balances from the old asset masters—including accumulated depreciation—should be transferred to the new asset masters. Additionally, the system should calculate and display planned depreciation values period-wise in the asset master.

To address this, I have taken the following steps:

  1. Created legacy assets under the new asset class using AS91, mirroring the existing assets.
  2. Used AS92 to take over values by entering acquisition value and accumulated depreciation.

While the net book value is appearing correctly, the planned depreciation is not being calculated as expected. Instead of distributing depreciation period-wise, the system is showing the remaining depreciation as a lump sum in the next period.

Could you please suggest the correct steps or procedure to meet this requirement?

Thanks in advance

FIN Asset Accounting 

@Lakshmipathi @Divyesh_Mistry
